Documentation.short_help: Decompose a tetrahedral mesh into regions by connectivity (graph min-cut).
Documentation.long_help: Takes a vtkUnstructuredGrid containing tetrahedra (e.g. a TetGen result) and tags every tetrahedron with a RegionId cell array. The dual graph treats each tet as a node and connects tets that share a triangular face. Connected Components labels each face-connected body. Balanced Min-Cut Bisection recursively splits the dual graph with a graph-theoretic minimum cut (Dinic max-flow / min-cut) so the number of cut shared faces (or their total area) between subdomains is minimized while the parts stay balanced. Non-tetrahedral cells are passed through with RegionId = -1.

IntVectorProperty.name: PartitionMethod
IntVectorProperty.label: Partition Method
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Connected Components: one region per face-connected body. Balanced Min-Cut Bisection: recursively bisect the dual graph with a built-in minimum cut (best cut quality, but does not scale to millions of tetrahedra). METIS k-way: multilevel partitioning via METIS (default, scales to millions of tets in seconds; run per connected component with CONTIG so regions stay connected).

IntVectorProperty.name: NumberOfRegions
IntVectorProperty.label: Number Of Regions
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Target number of regions for Balanced Min-Cut Bisection or METIS k-way. If the mesh has more connected components than this, the component count wins (regions never span disconnected bodies).

DoubleVectorProperty.name: BalanceBand
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Balance Band
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Fraction of nodes left free for the min-cut on each bisection (the rest are anchored half to each flow terminal by graph distance). 0 anchors everything and forces a balanced median split (the cut is not optimized); larger values give the min-cut more freedom to shrink the interface at the cost of balance (1 = pure global min-cut, smallest interface but possibly very unbalanced).

IntVectorProperty.name: UseFaceAreaWeights
IntVectorProperty.label: Use Face Area Weights
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When ON, edge weights are the shared-face areas, so the cut minimizes interface area. When OFF, each shared face has unit weight (minimize cut face count). Applies to both Balanced Min-Cut and METIS k-way.
